confront.title: Confront
confront.desc: You address conflict directly and seek resolution through open discussion. This clarifies issues fast and refuses festering — but in a team that needs safety, it can escalate faster than the situation warrants.
confront.watch: confronting becomes domination when there is no real listening behind the directness.

negotiate.title: Negotiate
negotiate.desc: You seek compromise: a middle ground where each side trades something. This preserves the relationship and gets movement — but on questions of principle, the compromise can gut both positions.
negotiate.fits: conflicts of interest where the relationship and the outcome both matter.
negotiate.watch: negotiation becomes transactional smoothing when it papers over a real disagreement.

accommodate.title: Accommodate
accommodate.desc: You yield to preserve the relationship and prioritize harmony. This builds goodwill and absorbs friction — but chronic accommodation accumulates resentment and lets bad patterns continue.
accommodate.fits: low-stakes conflicts where the relationship matters more than the point.
accommodate.watch: accommodation becomes self-erasure when the stakes rise and you still yield.

avoid.title: Avoid
avoid.desc: You withdraw or defer, minimizing engagement and waiting for the conflict to settle. This avoids impulsive escalation — but avoidance becomes a pattern when the conflict keeps recurring because it was never addressed.
avoid.fits: trivial conflicts, overheated moments, or bad timing for a decision.
avoid.watch: avoidance becomes neglect when important issues never get resolved.

transform.title: Transform
transform.desc: You reframe the conflict as a shared problem and seek a win-win that addresses the underlying need. This is the most durable fix — but it takes real time and skill, and it can become evasion when you lack the authority or honesty to do it.
transform.fits: complex, recurring conflicts where the surface disagreement hides a deeper one.
transform.watch: transformation becomes wishful reframing when the reframe does not actually change anyone’s incentives.

complement: If your primary is Confront, you are steadied by someone who Accommodates or Negotiates — but not fully Avoid. If your primary is Avoid, you need someone who Confronts productively before avoidance becomes neglect.

shareTexts.minimal: My conflict signature is {primary}. What is yours?
shareTexts.full:
My conflict signature is {primary}, with {secondary} close behind.
What is yours?

recommendation.label: Outfinity initiative
recommendation.title: Explorer Circle
recommendation.text: Explorer Circle is a community for builders comparing how they move through conflict and finding partners whose signatures complement rather than collide. You do not need to share your result to participate.
recommendation.note: Opening Explorer Circle takes you to Substack. Your result is not transmitted.
